s3.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s3.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s3.q: Which of the following best defines this condition?
options: A pathological deviation from normal anatomy or function
An incidental finding with no clinical significance
s3.explain: A definition describes the essential nature of a condition as a deviation from normal.

s3.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition
It is rarely relevant in clinical practice
It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s3.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

s3.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s3.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s3.q: The incidence of this condition is highest in which population?
options: Depends on the specific condition and risk factors
s3.explain: Incidence varies by condition — each has its own epidemiology and risk profile.

s3.q: What is the ultimate goal of antenatal care?
options: Ensure all pregnancies result in vaginal delivery
Reduce maternal and perinatal mortality and morbidity rates
Guarantee a healthy newborn in every pregnancy
s3.explain: The ultimate goal of antenatal care is to reduce maternal and perinatal mortality and morbidity rates, prepare the woman for labor, lactation, and care of her infant, and detect and treat complicated conditions early.

s3.q: Which of the following is NOT a way the goal of ANC is achieved?
options: Promotion of physical, mental, and social health of mother and baby
Development of birth preparedness and complication readiness plan
Scheduling routine cesarean delivery for all primigravida
s3.explain: The goal of ANC is achieved through detection and management of complications, promoting health, developing birth preparedness, and preparing the mother to breastfeed successfully. Routine C-section is not a goal of ANC.

s4.q: The clinical significance of this section is:
options: It applies only to research settings
s4.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s4.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s4.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s4.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s4.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s4.q: How many main components of antenatal care are listed?
s4.explain: The 8 components of ANC are: (1) Registration & history, (2) Periodic examination & lab, (3) High-risk pregnancy management, (4) Health education, (5) Nutritional care, (6) Emotional & social care, (7) Preventive measures (TT immunization), (8) Referral when needed.

s4.q: Which component of antenatal care includes tetanus toxoid immunization?
s4.explain: Preventive measures include tetanus toxoid immunization as a key component of antenatal care.

s5.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s5.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s5.q: During which gestational age should a pregnant woman visit every 2 weeks?
s5.explain: Standard visit frequency: Every 4 weeks till 28th week, every 2 weeks from 28th to 36th week, and every week from 36th week till labor.

s5.q: According to the minimum 8-visit schedule, when should GDM screening (50g GCT) be performed?
s5.explain: GDM screening using 50g GCT is performed at 24-28 weeks, which falls in the 20-26 week visit window (Visit 2-3).

options: It applies only to research settings
s6.explain: Understanding these concepts directly informs clinical management.

s6.q: Which of the following is listed as a barrier to effective antenatal care?
s6.explain: Barriers include: poor access, poverty, cultural norms (women needing spouses' consent), poor quality perception, and inadequate facilities.

s6.q: How does poor quality perception act as a barrier to ANC?
options: Prolonged waiting time and unprofessional conduct discourage women from seeking care
It increases the cost of services
It reduces the number of available medications
s6.explain: Prolonged outpatient waiting time and unprofessional conduct of service providers create a perception of poor quality that discourages utilization.

options: It applies only to research settings

s7.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s7.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s7.q: How is the Expected Date of Delivery (EDD) calculated using Naegele's rule?
options: Add 9 months and 7 days to LMP
Subtract 3 months and add 7 days to the first day of LMP
Add 280 days to the date of conception
Subtract 1 month and add 14 days to LMP
s7.explain: Naegele's rule: subtract 3 months, add 7 days to the first day of LMP.

s7.q: At what gestational age can fetal heart tones be auscultated using a Doppler?
s7.explain: Fetal heart tones can be auscultated with Doppler at 10-12 weeks, and with Pinard's stethoscope after 20 weeks.

s8.q: What is the recommended total weight gain by the 20th week of pregnancy?
s8.explain: At 20th week approximately 3.5 kg is gained, then about 0.5 kg/week. Total recommended: 11-16 kg.

s8.q: When should Leopold maneuvers to assess fetal presentation begin?
s8.explain: Leopold maneuvers for abdominal palpation to assess fetal presentation begin at 36 weeks' gestation.

s8.q: Which screening test for trisomy and NTD is performed at 15-20 weeks?
s8.explain: Trisomy & NTD screening at 15-20 weeks uses triple screen (HCG, unconjugated estriol, α-fetoprotein) or quadruple screen (+ inhibin-A).

s9.q: In obstetric practice, this knowledge helps:
s9.explain: Comprehensive knowledge enables prevention, early diagnosis, and optimal management.

s9.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s9.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s9.q: What is the recommended total weight gain range for a pregnant woman with a healthy pre-pregnancy BMI (18.5-24.9)?
s9.explain: For healthy weight (BMI 18.5-24.9), the recommended total weight gain is 11.5-16 kg.

s9.q: An obese pregnant woman (BMI ≥ 30) should gain how much weight during pregnancy?
s9.explain: For obese women (BMI ≥ 30), the recommended total weight gain is 5-9 kg.

options: It is essential for understanding the condition

It applies only to specific populations
It has been replaced by newer classifications
s10.explain: Understanding key concepts is fundamental to clinical management.

options: Depends on the specific condition and severity
s10.explain: Management approach varies — some conditions require surgery, others medical or conservative care.

s10.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s10.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s10.q: Which of the following is a risk factor detected from history that indicates high-risk pregnancy?
s10.explain: Risk factors from history include: mother's age 35 years, primigravida or grand multipara, birth spacing <2 years, previous CS, chronic diseases, etc.

s10.q: Which of the following is a risk factor detected late in pregnancy?
s10.explain: Risk factors detected late in pregnancy include: severe PIH, antepartum hemorrhage, multiple pregnancy, IUGR, and abnormal presentation.

s11.q: How many doses of tetanus toxoid are recommended for a pregnant woman in her first pregnancy?
s11.explain: Two doses of TT with 4 weeks apart are recommended. For subsequent pregnancies, one dose is given.

s11.q: Which vaccine is strongly recommended during pregnancy according to the content?
s11.explain: COVID-19 vaccines are strongly recommended in pregnancy for best protection against known risks including ICU admission and premature birth.

s12.q: What is the best sleeping position recommended during pregnancy?
s12.explain: The best sleeping position during pregnancy is Sims' position.

s12.q: A pregnant woman should be advised to avoid all of the following EXCEPT:
s12.explain: Regular moderate exercise like walking (at least 30 min) is recommended. Hot baths, tight clothes, and prolonged standing should be avoided.

s12.q: Smoking during pregnancy increases the risk of all EXCEPT:
s12.explain: Smoking increases risk of preterm birth, LBW, cleft lip/palate, and SIDS.
